meta_title: Impact Doors in Laud Lakes, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Protect your Laud Lakes home with impact doors from Premier Impact Windows & Roofing. Hurricane-rated entry, sliding & French doors. Licensed, insured, A+ BBB. Free quotes!
h1: Impact Doors in Laud Lakes, FL — Built to Guard What Matters Most
intro:
Laud Lakes may sit about eight miles inland from the Atlantic coastline, but that distance offers far less protection than most homeowners realize. When a major hurricane makes landfall near South Florida, wind speeds at inland Broward County communities like Laud Lakes can still reach the 170-mph threshold that defines this area's wind zone classification — more than enough force to blow out a standard entry door, shatter an ordinary sliding glass door, or compromise the structural integrity of an entire home. For the families living throughout neighborhoods like Lauderdale Lakes North, upgrading to certified impact doors isn't a luxury upgrade — it's a smart, necessary investment in safety, security, and long-term home value.

With roughly 55% of homes in the Laud Lakes area built before 1994 — the year that triggered sweeping changes to Florida's building codes in the aftermath of Hurricane Andrew — there are thousands of properties in and around ZIP code 33319 that still rely on original exterior doors never designed to handle today's code-required wind loads. These older entry doors, sliding glass panels, and French door systems were installed in an era when the catastrophic lessons of Andrew had not yet been written into law. If your home falls into that pre-1994 category, you may be living with a significant structural vulnerability every time a named storm enters the Gulf or the Atlantic.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we've spent decades helping South Florida homeowners close that gap. Known throughout Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties as 'The Kings of Service,' our team brings 60-plus years of combined installation experience to every project we take on. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door near Lake Side Park or retrofitting every exterior opening in your home with impact-resistant systems, we deliver the craftsmanship, code compliance, and customer care that Laud Lakes homeowners deserve. With average home values around $270,000, every smart improvement you make protects real financial equity — and a new set of impact doors is one of the highest-return upgrades available.

section_why.h2: Why Laud Lakes Homeowners Trust Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
Choosing an impact door installer in South Florida isn't just about finding the lowest price — it's about finding a company with the credentials, local experience, and commitment to quality that will stand behind their work long after the installation truck leaves your driveway. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation as 'The Kings of Service' by doing exactly that across hundreds of homes in Broward County and beyond. Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, 500-plus completed homes, and fully licensed and insured status aren't just marketing badges — they're the result of consistently doing right by real homeowners in real communities like Laud Lakes.

One of the most important things that sets us apart from many competitors is our 100% in-house installation model. We never subcontract your job to an unknown third party. Every member of our installation crew is a trained Premier Impact Windows & Roofing employee who knows our products, understands Broward County permitting requirements, and takes personal pride in the quality of each completed install. When you invest in impact doors for your Laud Lakes home, you deserve to know exactly who is on your property and that they're accountable to the same standards from start to finish.

Our team's 60-plus years of combined experience means we've seen virtually every door configuration, framing condition, and installation challenge that South Florida homes can present — including the quirks common to the pre-1994 construction stock that makes up a majority of homes in the Laud Lakes area. Older homes often require additional prep work, specialized framing adjustments, or careful attention to threshold and weatherproofing details that newer construction doesn't. We know how to handle those situations efficiently and correctly, so your new impact doors perform exactly as they're engineered to — from the first storm season and every one that follows.

From the initial consultation and product selection through the permit filing with the Broward County Building Division, the physical installation, the final inspection, and the post-project walkthrough, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages every step of your project with transparency and professionalism. We work around your schedule, communicate clearly at every phase, and make sure you understand exactly what was installed and why it meets or exceeds the applicable codes for your Laud Lakes home.

section_history.h2: A Proven Storm Threat: Why Laud Lakes Has Learned to Take Hurricanes Seriously
section_history.body:
Laud Lakes and the surrounding Broward County communities have been tested by major storms nine times since 1990, and each event has reinforced the same hard lesson: no inland address is automatically safe during hurricane season. The most formative of those events is Hurricane Andrew in 1992, which struck southern Miami-Dade County with catastrophic force but sent damaging winds well north into Broward. For a community where more than half the existing homes were already built by that point, Andrew was a wake-up call about how poorly older door and window systems performed under serious wind pressure. The code reforms that followed in 1994 were directly inspired by the destruction Andrew caused, and they permanently changed what 'adequate' hurricane protection means in South Florida.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 delivered another punishing reminder to Broward County residents. Wilma crossed Florida from the Gulf side and tracked directly through the county, producing widespread wind damage, extended power outages, and significant destruction to homes that still hadn't been upgraded since Andrew. Laud Lakes was not spared. Homeowners with standard doors reported entry failures, blown seals, and water intrusion that caused far more interior damage than the wind alone. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which brought strong tropical-storm-to-hurricane-force winds across the entire county and once again demonstrated that even a storm that doesn't make a direct hit near your ZIP code can compromise unprotected door openings.

That pattern of repeated storm exposure — nine significant events since 1990 — is precisely why impact door upgrades in Laud Lakes are not optional for homeowners who take storm preparedness seriously. Every hurricane season runs from June through November, and every year that passes without upgrading to impact-rated doors is another season of unnecessary risk for your family and your property.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code & Broward County Requirements for Impact Doors in Laud Lakes
section_code.body:
Laud Lakes falls within Broward County's jurisdiction for building code enforcement, which means all exterior door replacements are subject to the Florida Building Code — a set of standards that has been continuously strengthened since the catastrophic failures documented during Hurricane Andrew. While Laud Lakes is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that applies to areas closer to the coast, it is still required by state code to use hurricane-rated products that meet strict design pressure and impact-resistance standards. This is a common misconception among inland homeowners: being outside the HVHZ does not mean you can install standard, non-rated doors. The 170-mph wind zone designation covering this area makes impact certification a legal requirement, not just a recommendation.

All impact door products installed in Laud Lakes must carry a valid Florida Product Approval (FPA) number demonstrating they've been tested and approved to withstand the specific design pressures associated with Broward County's wind zone. Entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors must all meet these requirements independently. The approval process is rigorous — products are subjected to large-missile impact testing, pressure cycling, and water infiltration testing before they earn FPA status. When Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs a door in your Laud Lakes home, every product we use carries current Florida Product Approval documentation.

Permit filing goes through the Broward County Building Division, and every legitimate impact door installation in Laud Lakes requires a permit before work begins. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles all permit applications on your behalf, coordinates the required inspections, and ensures that your project is fully documented and closed out correctly. This matters not only for legal compliance but also for your homeowner's insurance policy, your wind mitigation report, and any future real estate transaction involving your property. A fully permitted impact door installation is a documented, verifiable upgrade — and that documentation has real monetary value.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium Protection for Laud Lakes Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the most respected impact door product lines available in South Florida: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are known for engineering products that meet or exceed Florida's demanding building code requirements while delivering the aesthetics and functionality that today's homeowners expect. For Laud Lakes residents looking to upgrade their entry doors, sliding glass doors, or French doors, these product lines offer a compelling combination of certified storm protection, modern design options, and long-term durability suited to South Florida's climate.

ESW impact doors are designed specifically for Florida's high-wind environment and carry Florida Product Approval for use in Broward County's wind zone. Their entry door systems feature heavy-duty multi-point locking mechanisms that engage the frame at multiple points simultaneously — a critical feature that dramatically increases resistance to wind pressure compared to traditional single-point locks. Multi-point locking isn't just a security upgrade; it's a structural one. When a door is locked at three, five, or more points along its height, it behaves more like a solid wall panel under wind loading rather than a hinged panel trying to resist force at a single latch point. ESW's sliding glass door and French door systems are equally engineered for performance, featuring laminated impact glass that stays intact even when struck by windborne debris.

CWI products bring similar engineering credentials and a reputation for quality hardware and refined finishes that appeal to homeowners who want impact protection without compromising their home's curb appeal. CWI's door systems are available in a range of configurations and finishes suitable for the architectural styles common throughout Lauderdale Lakes North and the broader Laud Lakes community. Whether you're replacing a dated aluminum slider or upgrading to a grand double French door entry, both ESW and CWI give you the certified performance, visual appeal, and long-term reliability that your Laud Lakes home deserves.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Doors Cost in Laud Lakes — and How to Make Them Affordable
section_cost.body:
For homeowners in Laud Lakes, where the average home value sits around $270,000, the cost of impact door installation represents a meaningful but highly strategic investment. Impact entry door replacements in South Florida typically range from $1,500 to $4,000 per door depending on size, configuration, hardware, and finish selections, while sliding glass door systems and French doors can range from $2,500 to $7,000 or more based on panel count and design complexity. A full-home door package for a typical Laud Lakes residence might run anywhere from $5,000 to $15,000 depending on the number and type of openings involved — a figure that sounds significant until you weigh it against what it protects.

Consider that a single hurricane event causing interior water damage through a failed door can easily produce $10,000 to $30,000 in repair costs — often not fully covered by insurance depending on your policy terms and deductible structure. When you frame impact door investment as storm damage insurance, the numbers shift considerably. Add in the insurance premium savings available to Broward County homeowners with verified impact openings (more on that below), and many homeowners find that their net out-of-pocket cost over a five-to-ten-year horizon is substantially lower than the sticker price suggests.

To make impact doors accessible for every budget level in Laud Lakes, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options starting as low as $87 per month with $0 down available for qualified applicants. Our financing partners work with a range of credit profiles, and our team will help you find a payment structure that makes sense for your household. For a lower-middle-income community like Laud Lakes, we believe that storm protection shouldn't be a privilege reserved for high-income zip codes — it should be achievable for every family willing to take that step, and our financing options are designed to make that possible.

section_insurance.h2: Homeowner's Insurance Savings for Laud Lakes Impact Door Upgrades
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially compelling reasons to upgrade to impact doors in Laud Lakes is the potential for significant homeowner's insurance savings — savings that many Broward County residents don't fully understand or take advantage of. Florida's wind mitigation credit system, established under state law, requires insurance carriers to offer premium discounts to policyholders who can demonstrate that their home has qualifying opening protections. A certified wind mitigation inspection documents your impact doors and windows, and that report goes directly to your insurer as the basis for calculating credits on your windstorm premium.

For Laud Lakes homeowners with Citizens Property Insurance, State Farm, Universal Property, Heritage, or any of the many carriers active in Broward County, wind mitigation credits for full impact opening protection can reduce the wind portion of your annual premium by 20% to 45% or more depending on your specific policy and the scope of your upgrades. On a home with a $270,000 value carrying a typical South Florida windstorm premium, those savings can translate to several hundred dollars per year — year after year. Over a decade, the cumulative insurance savings alone can offset a substantial portion of your initial door investment.

The key to maximizing your savings is ensuring that every door installation is properly permitted, inspected, and documented. This is another reason why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's commitment to full permit compliance matters: without a completed permit and a passing inspection, your wind mitigation report cannot accurately credit your impact doors, and you leave money on the table every renewal cycle. Our team will also advise you on scheduling a wind mitigation inspection after your installation is complete so you can submit your updated report to your insurance carrier and begin capturing those savings as quickly as possible.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact doors in Laud Lakes if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Laud Lakes carries a 170-mph wind zone designation under the Florida Building Code, which means code-compliant impact-rated products are legally required for any door replacement — regardless of coastal proximity. Inland locations are not immune to hurricane-force winds; Hurricanes Andrew, Wilma, and Irma all produced damaging wind speeds in this area. Impact doors protect your home's structural integrity and your family's safety whether you're on the beach or eight miles inland.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors typically cost for a Laud Lakes home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door costs in Laud Lakes typically range from $1,500 to $4,000 for a standard entry door and $2,500 to $7,000 or more for sliding glass or French door systems. A full-home package often runs $5,000 to $15,000 depending on scope. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified applicants, making it accessible for a wide range of household budgets throughout the Laud Lakes community.

faqs.q: What permits are required for impact door installation in Laud Lakes, and who handles them?
faqs.a: All impact door replacements in Laud Lakes require a building permit through the Broward County Building Division before work begins. The permit process involves plan review, product approval documentation, and at least one field inspection upon completion. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf — application, documentation, scheduling inspections, and ensuring the project is properly closed out. You never have to navigate the permit system yourself.

faqs.q: How long does an impact door installation take for a typical Laud Lakes home?
faqs.a: Once your permit is approved by the Broward County Building Division, the physical installation of impact doors in a standard Laud Lakes home typically takes one to two days depending on the number of openings. A single entry door can often be completed in a few hours. The full project timeline from initial consultation to completed inspection generally runs four to eight weeks, with most of that time consumed by permit processing and product lead times rather than the installation itself.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Laud Lakes?
faqs.a: Yes — in most cases, meaningfully so. Florida law requires carriers to offer wind mitigation credits to policyholders with certified impact opening protection. After your Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installation is complete and permitted, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your upgrades and provide a report to your insurer. Broward County homeowners with full impact protection commonly see windstorm premium reductions of 20% to 45%, potentially saving several hundred dollars annually over the life of your policy.
